title: Fix Inverted Cursor Behavior in Multi-Select Dropdown Checkboxes
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want the cursor to correctly show a pointer over checkbox labels in dropdowns and a default cursor over the dropdown item background, so that clickable affordances are intuitive.
description:
The multi-select filter dropdown had inverted cursor behavior: hovering over the checkbox showed a pointer cursor (hand), while hovering over the checkbox label showed a default cursor. This was backwards from the expected behavior.

### CSS Fix
In `filter_multi_select.css`:
- `.filter-multi-select .dropdown-item`: set `cursor: default`
- `.filter-multi-select .dropdown-item .custom-control-label`: set `cursor: pointer`

The fix was applied in the custom CSS file rather than modifying the third-party library's core CSS.
acceptance_criteria: Hovering over a dropdown item background shows a default cursor
Hovering over a checkbox label in the dropdown shows a pointer cursor
CSS overrides are in custom.css, not in the third-party library files
ux_changes: Fixed inverted cursor behavior in multi-select filter dropdowns. Checkbox labels now correctly show a pointer cursor, while the dropdown item background shows a default cursor.
implementation_notes: CSS overrides were placed in custom.css instead of modifying the third-party library's core filter_multi_select.css to avoid issues with future library updates.
comment_summary: Developer applied the CSS fix in custom.css rather than modifying the library core file. Change was verified on staging in incognito mode.
